mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A sustained 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through lower cost-of-capital effects.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.42% as of April 2019 reflects accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days turn negative as equity investors anticipate earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of April 2019 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite UNRATE spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity investors expect earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side momentum that single-series analysis misses.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513,356M and HOUST at 1,139K units as of March 2019
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when both RSAFS and HOUST grow are not significantly higher than when only one grows (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings and inflation expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite INDPRO weakness, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) interact to signal wage-price spiral risk. When PAYEMS growth accelerates above 200K monthly while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed faces tightening pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,095K and CPIAUCSL at 254.96 as of April 2019
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both conditions trigger are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as an always-on trend signal. Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as sustained economic expansion supports corporate earnings.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than low-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interact to signal real rate regime shifts. When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains flat or declines, real rates compress, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity discount rates fall in real terms.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.2% and FEDFUNDS unchanged or declining
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 254.96 and FEDFUNDS at 2.42% as of April 2019
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both conditions trigger are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) acceleration above 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side strength and productivity gains. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice profit margins upward in response to improved operational efficiency.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite INDPRO strength, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.
